content.body:
Charged for 8 months on the wrong plan — and refused a refund
After my trial ended, Easyship placed me on a Premier subscription at £82.80/month without my knowledge. Their own email to me at the time stated my plan was Plus — not Premier. When I queried the charges, they acknowledged the email was an error on their part, but still refused to issue a refund, citing their T&Cs.
We send around 20 parcels a month. There is no world in which we would have knowingly signed up to Premier. The miscommunication from their own team is the reason I didn't question the charges sooner, and they've admitted as much — yet won't take responsibility for it.
I'm now pursuing a chargeback through my bank. I'd strongly advise anyone signing up to Easyship to check their billing carefully, as it appears plan upgrades can happen without clear consent.

classification.summary: Customer was incorrectly charged for 8 months on a higher-tier plan without consent and is seeking alternative shipping providers after the company refused to refund the error.

content.body:
We’ve been using Easyship for 1+ year now to manage global shipping for our hardware product, and it has become a core part of our operations.
- Their platform stands out for how well it handles complex shipping logic.- The rules builder allows us to control routing across multiple warehouses and carriers with precision- Overall while some carriers might have limited tracking the choice is ample and it allows to optimize for cheaper, fastest or best value delivery, and lets our clients choose what they prefer.- The integrations (multiple warehouses) were seamless for us. Connecting Easyship with Shopify and our warehouse systems was straightforward, and it enabled us to automate label generation, tracking, and customer notifications without additional overhead.- Support: while sometimes their team would need to clarify exact issues with carriers, especially when Customs delay is the case, they are very responsive and always available on live chat. Phone support is also available but in general chat support resolves the case.
Overall, Easyship provides the infrastructure needed to run global logistics efficiently, without unnecessary complexity.

classification.summary: A satisfied Easyship customer in Germany reviews their positive experience with the platform's shipping logic, warehouse routing, and integration capabilities.

Domestic UK shipping is ok - International shipments not ok. It told me my international shipments would use my iOSS number in the interface, but it didn't and all my customers had customs bills. This made them angry and wasted a lot of admin time/cash flow issuing refunds.
Customer service via email was slow and useless, it took days to get a response, and the response wasn't helpful. If you want to speak to somebody on the phone you have to upgrade your plan which you shouldn't have to do. Why would you want to spend more money to fix a basic issue?
I then had a sales person phone me to ask if I wanted to upgrade, I said no as I was having issues, he was like ok bye LOL.
Anyway CBA with this anymore, business running is hard enough without tools making it harder. I've ended up using Royal Mail Click + Drop directly as it's more reliable.
Constructive feedback - work on your customer service, if you'd have helped, I would have stayed.

content.body:
Over the past three years, ShipStation has become an essential part of our operation. The efficiency gains alone have been a game changer—what used to take hours each day is now streamlined into a much faster, more organized process. From batch label creation to automated workflows, it has significantly reduced manual work and allowed our team to focus on higher-value tasks.
As our business has grown, ShipStation has scaled right alongside us. It handles multi-channel orders seamlessly and gives us the flexibility to customize workflows based on our specific needs. The integrations are smooth, and everything from order syncing to tracking updates just works reliably.
What really stands out is how powerful yet approachable the platform is. It strikes a great balance between being easy to use day-to-day while still offering the advanced automation needed for a growing business. It has helped us improve fulfillment speed, reduce errors, and ultimately operate more efficiently across the board.
Overall, ShipStation has proven to be a dependable, scalable shipping solution that delivers on automation, integrations, and efficiency. It’s been a key tool in supporting our growth and simplifying what used to be a very complex part of the business.

metadata.owner_response:
Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We take concerns involving mail or security matters very seriously.

Cubework operates shared commercial facilities, and mail or deliveries addressed to individuals or businesses may be intended for independent tenants operating within the building. Because of this, we may not always have visibility into items addressed to specific individuals.

If you believe there has been a security or fraud-related issue, we recommend continuing to work with the appropriate authorities. You may also contact our team through our website at https://www.cubework.com/contact
 so we can review the matter internally.

Thank you for notifying us.

metadata.owner_response:
Filtasen, we take feedback seriously. Reliability, accuracy, and transparency are core to how we operate here at ShipMonk. The concerns you’ve raised -- including order accuracy, carrier performance, invoicing, and offboarding costs -- are all areas our teams actively monitor and continuously improve.

Regarding cut-off times and carrier selection, we offer multiple service levels and shipping options designed to balance speed and cost. Some of our merchants choose economy services, but expedited options are available. If expectations around transit times were not aligned, that’s something we want to review directly with you.

On invoicing and system performance, our billing is fully itemized, and our platform processes millions of transactions each month. If discrepancies occur, our finance and support teams investigate and resolve them promptly. We do not tolerate systemic billing inaccuracies.

Most importantly, order accuracy and protecting our merchants’ customer relationships are non-negotiable priorities for us. If there were packing errors that impacted subscriptions, we want to thoroughly audit those orders to help make it right.

Our reputation is built on long-term partnerships and measurable performance outcomes. We welcome the opportunity to review your account in detail and address any outstanding concerns. Please reach out to us directly at support@shipmonk.com with your company name so our leadership team can engage.

We remain committed to accountability, transparency, and continuous improvement -- and to earning the trust of every merchant we serve.

content.body:
BEWARE!
We started fulfilling through ShipBob in October 2025 and it has been the worst decision we have made since launching the business. One of the carriers that was included in the ship-option was Amazon Shipping. ShipBob has shipped so many (at least 50%) of our orders via Amazon Shipping and it has been an absolute nightmare! All of our inventory is at ShipBob's FC in Las Vegas and all ordered shipped through Amazon Shipping initially travel to San Bernandino, CA. Most orders sit in San Bernandino for a few days without any updates. That is the case even if the shipping address is southern California. For East Coast shipping addresses, they eventually start moving east (that's if they ever start moving). Even if the final destination is Texas, Amazon will move the packages to Alabama or Tennessee. The packages will then sit there for another few days. In the meantime, customers are furious that Amazon either provides no updates or they never deliver. We then reach out to ShipBob for answers except ShipBob never escalates the issues to Amazon for updates.
After weeks of complaining to ShipBob and asking them to remove Amazon Shipping from our carriers list, they finally respond and suggest a new rate sheet that will literally cost us 22.47% more to exclude Amazon from the list. We are speechless! This was the response after ShipBob actively pushed us out for several weeks while we were dealing with customer complaints and having to ship replacement units at our own expense (because ShipBob has a 30-45 day window you have to wait for before you become eligible to file a claim).
We are scaling quickly and ShipBob has done a good job losing the opportunity to build a long term relationship with us.
p.s. Good luck trying to reach the salesperson at ShipBob for support. They disappear once you sign up with them.

classification.summary: 13 Under Golf is actively looking to switch from ShipBob due to poor carrier performance (Amazon Shipping), lack of support, excessive costs to exclude problematic carriers, and slow order fulfillment causing customer dissatisfaction.

content.body:
I wish I could give less than one star.

I visited WareSpace today and had one of the worst customer experiences I’ve ever encountered — especially as a business owner looking to invest money.

Background: We operate an AV/Production company and would be storing hundreds of thousands of dollars’ worth of equipment in this space. Security, professionalism, and customer service are critical for us.

When I arrived, I rang the front door bell and was immediately met with an aggressive voice practically yelling at me for ringing it. I then realized I had accidentally scheduled my tour for next Monday instead of today — that part was my mistake.
However, what happened next was even more concerning.
As I walked back to my car, I noticed a back door to the facility standing wide open. Naturally, as a prospective customer considering storing high-value equipment, I stepped inside to briefly look around and get a feel for the space.

I was immediately approached by the “Maintenance Manager,” who began scolding me — while wearing AirPods during the interaction. It was difficult to even follow what he was saying. The tone was unprofessional, hostile, and completely lacking basic customer service.

I had driven 20 minutes to view a facility I was prepared to spend significant money with. Instead of being met with courtesy or professionalism, I was treated like an inconvenience.
For a company that markets itself toward entrepreneurs and growing businesses, this was extremely disappointing.

Needless to say, we will be taking our business elsewhere.

classification.summary: A business owner with high-value equipment storage needs experienced hostile and unprofessional customer service at a warehouse facility and is now seeking an alternative provider.

metadata.place_name: WareSpace Downers Grove | Small Warehouse Space Rentals
metadata.place_brand: WareSpace Downers Grove | Small Warehouse Space Rentals

content.body:
We wanted to update this review now that our insurance claim has finally been resolved as of February 2026.
In September 2025, a high-value coffee table we shipped via EasyShip was delivered damaged. We submitted an insurance claim immediately. Unfortunately, over the following months, communication was extremely limited, progress was unclear, and we were repeatedly told that “multiple parties were involved” without being given any meaningful updates or evidence from the carrier.
Despite numerous follow-ups, the claim remained unresolved for several months. We were eventually informed that compensation would not be paid, and only a partial goodwill refund was offered. At that stage, we had little option but to issue a formal Money Claim Online (MCOL) claim to recover our losses.
Only after legal proceedings were started did EasyShip approve payment of the insured value.
Even then, payment was initially issued as platform credit rather than funds, and multiple attempts to transfer the balance to our bank failed or were reversed, causing further delays. A formal settlement agreement was eventually required before the final balance was paid.
While we did ultimately receive the insured value of the goods, we were left out of pocket for unrecovered legal and administrative costs. We were also previously offered incentives to amend or remove our review before the matter was resolved, which we found concerning.
We appreciate that individual staff members did try to help towards the end of the process, and we are glad the claim was eventually settled. However, the lack of transparency, prolonged delays, and the need for legal action to achieve a basic insurance payout made this an extremely stressful and time-consuming experience.
For businesses shipping high-value items, this is something to be aware of. We would strongly recommend carefully considering whether you are comfortable relying on EasyShip’s insurance and claims process.

classification.summary: User experienced severe delays and poor communication with EasyShip's insurance claims process for a damaged high-value shipment, requiring legal action to recover losses, and is warning other businesses about the unreliability of their insurance services.

content.body:
ShipStation came to our company's rescue.  We previously used Shopify's native shipping to process our orders.  When we experienced an overnight surge of hundreds of thousands of orders, We immediately needed a solution to ship in bulk and batch orders for efficiency. I frantically called ShipStation and their team instantly sprang into action and treated our challenge with urgency, care, and true partnership. Stephani Williams (Implementation Specialist) and her team—along with James and Maia from Product Management—were nothing short of exceptional during one of the most critical moments in our company’s growth.
Stephani’s leadership was outstanding. She coordinated multiple on-site visits, worked hands-on with our team to identify and resolve operational kinks, and ensured that every detail of our implementation was optimized for scale. James and Maia brought deep product expertise, helping us navigate complex workflows and tailor solutions that fit our unique needs. Together, they made an overwhelming situation feel manageable and ultimately successful.
What truly sets this team apart is their dedication. They checked in regularly, were always available to answer questions, and proactively suggested improvements that strengthened our operations even further. Thanks to their commitment and expertise, we were able to get up and running quickly and confidently handle a massive increase in volume.
We are incredibly grateful for ShipStation's partnership and cannot recommend Stephani, James, Maia, and the entire ShipStation team highly enough. They didn’t just implement a solution—they became a trusted extension of our business.

classification.summary: A company successfully scaled from hundreds of thousands of overnight orders using ShipStation's bulk shipping and batch processing capabilities with exceptional implementation support.

content.body: I'm going to keep posting this review everyone on every single one of my accounts in the hopes that this can save someone serious frustration. Steer clear. I've been with shipstation for over 6 years. Spent hundreds of thousands of dollars with 5+ different businesses. The app has had historic issues, glitches, etc. but it's never been detrimental is the probably 100K+ orders i've shipped out. I recently had an issue where I my default UPS account stopped working. I heavily rely on UPS as some of my customers cannot receive packages via USPS. It would not allow me to turn UPS on as an option (greyed out). I thought ok, I guess I'll start a ticket. It's now been three weeks, three excruciation weeks. Zero semblance of a solution. I've reached out probably 20 times. I get a generic response "don't worry we are looking into it" I've stressed how urgent it is. Finally after two weeks, they tell me they fixed, it - nope they actually just entirely DELETED ups as an option. It's nowhere to be found now on my dashboard and settings, the exact OPPOSITE of what i asked them to do. I reached back out several times, and asked it to be elevated, asked to speak to someone else. I even spent time to go on linkedin and try to find contact info for their exec team. I mean literally no one will help. It's honestly the worst customer service experience I've ever had in my life. If only I could share the back and forth. I feel unreliably frustrated and defeated here. It's hard to believe that this is as big of a company as it is. Rest assured, it may work for you right now, but if you EVER have issues, they simply don't care. Whether you are a large account or small, they will just ignore you and clearly have no processes in place to help people. It's such a shame.

classification.summary: Long-time ShipStation customer with 100K+ orders is actively seeking alternatives due to critical UPS integration failure and unresponsive customer support that made the problem worse.

content.body:
Working with ShipMonk has been a very poor experience and, frankly, it's a risky choice for businesses.

In my case, I submitted an order that needed to arrive in a specific day. The order was properly submitted in their system, met their stated cut off times, and was confirmed as correct. But the order did not ship. Later, the ShipMonk representative acknowledged that an internal mistake or delay on their side was the reason why the package sat for more than three days without being shipped.

This caused me to miss a paid event where I was supposed to sell my products. I had already paid the registration fee, and due to ShipMonk’s negligence, I lost the money and an important business opportunity.

Since the shipment was not arriving for the event, I requested a return to sender. That process took more than two weeks to even appear in the system, during which time no one could tell me where my package was.

After weeks of uncertainty, I was finally told that they “missed” the fact that the carrier had issued a new tracking number and only then did they realize the package was on its way back.

This level of negligence, lack of ownership, and poor communication puts businesses at risk. Not only operationally, but also commercially, because it can irreversibly harm your credibility with your customers, specially if you want to work in a B2B model. Based on my experience, I would not recommend ShipMonk to any business.

classification.summary: Dissatisfied ShipMonk customer experienced significant operational failures and missed business opportunities due to shipping delays and poor communication, actively seeking alternative fulfillment providers.

content.body:
I rarely leave negative reviews. If you look at my review history, you’ll see that I’m generally thoughtful, fair, and patient when issues arise. It takes an ongoing pattern of problems, combined with an unproductive management response, for me to feel compelled to write something like this.

This review is specific to the ReadySpaces location on Alma Ave in San Jose, and does not reflect on other ReadySpaces locations.

My experience at this Alma Ave facility has been disappointing, primarily due to management practices rather than the concept of the space itself.

The warehouse units at this location are very cold, especially for anyone working long hours or late into the evening. Despite this, there was never any written or verbal notice that space heaters were prohibited. That restriction does not appear in the license agreement or the facility rules provided for this site. When electrical issues occurred, the response from regional management focused on attributing fault to individual tenants rather than addressing infrastructure capacity or clearly communicating policy expectations.

Access to shared resources at this location has also been an issue. I was told multiple times that I did not have working access to the conference room reservation system, which made it impossible to reserve the room properly. At the same time, the conference room was being used for extended periods late into the night by the same unvetted group, effectively blocking access for other members. When shared spaces can be occupied indefinitely without oversight, it creates an inequitable environment.

I also raised concerns about non-business activity occurring in the facility during late hours at this location, based on direct observations. Surveillance cameras are present, yet these concerns were not meaningfully addressed, which raised questions about consistency in enforcement and after-hours oversight.

The most frustrating part has been the response from regional management overseeing this site. Communication from Ryan, the Regional Manager, came across as defensive and dismissive rather than collaborative or solution-oriented. Concerns were framed as tenant responsibility instead of being acknowledged as operational issues affecting members’ ability to work safely and productively.

For clarity and fairness, this review has no bearing on Property Manager Jonathon at this location, who has consistently been helpful, responsive, and compassionate within the limits of his role.

I would strongly encourage prospective tenants considering the Alma Ave ReadySpaces location, particularly womem, to ask very direct questions about temperature conditions, after-hours monitoring, shared space enforcement, and how tenant concerns are handled before committing. Transparency, consistency, and professionalism matter just as much as square footage, and those elements have been lacking in my experience at this specific location.

classification.summary: A long-term tenant is frustrated with management practices at a co-working facility including inadequate temperature control policies, inequitable access to shared resources, and unaddressed security concerns.

content.body:
I never expected to write a review like this. I am not angry and I am not trying to cause problems. I am sharing what happened because it was serious and because I wish someone had warned me.

A new business moved in next to a friend’s unit. After that, there were ongoing issues late at night. Strong chemical smells moved through the shared air, loud music played for hours, and animals were running around inside the building. My friend had about fifty thousand dollars of imported food stored there and was worried it could be ruined. Because of the environment, we made an informal swap. He used my space and I rented a small office instead.

Management was informed many times. The only response was adding a nighttime security guard, which tenants were required to help pay for. Nothing about the underlying situation changed.

Shortly before Christmas, I heard what sounded like animals fighting. When I investigated, I found a woman fully on fire. I used a fire extinguisher to put her out and called first responders. I stayed with her until help arrived. She did not survive. This experience was extremely traumatic and has required extensive therapy. I am not sure I will ever feel fully normal again.

After this, I asked for help covering about twelve hundred dollars in therapy copays related to what happened. Instead, I felt dismissed and shut down. I was told the incident did not happen, that video footage had been deleted, and I ran into resistance trying to obtain the police report. Multiple reviews describing this experience were also removed.

I reached out through many channels, including leadership, simply hoping to be treated like a person who went through something horrific. The response felt focused on protecting the company rather than acknowledging what occurred.

I truly hope this company grows in compassion and accountability. I am not trying to harm anyone or cause drama. I am sharing this so others are aware that if something serious happens, you may not receive the support you expect.

Please read this as one person’s lived experience and proceed with awareness. Image appears to be a scalp found a week after the incident.

classification.summary: A tenant experienced a fatal incident at a co-warehousing facility and feels the management prioritized protecting the company over acknowledging the tragedy and providing basic support.

content.body:
We’ve used a lot of warehouse tools over the years, and ShipHero stands out as one of the few platforms that’s clearly built by people who actually understand fulfillment at scale.
What separates ShipHero is how operationally thoughtful it is. The picking and packing workflows, batching logic, inventory controls, and error-reduction features aren’t fluff — they’re designed for speed, accuracy, and accountability. Once properly configured, it forces discipline into your warehouse in a way spreadsheets and lighter WMS tools simply can’t.
The system scales with you. As volume grows, ShipHero doesn’t break — it gets more valuable. We’ve been able to build repeatable processes, real KPIs, and clear ownership across our team, which has directly improved fulfillment speed and reduced costly mistakes.
Support has also been solid. When issues come up, the team understands warehouse reality and doesn’t give generic SaaS answers. That matters when orders are moving in real time.
ShipHero isn’t the cheapest option — and that’s a good thing. It’s not meant for early-stage brands that want shortcuts. It’s for operators who care about precision, scalability, and building a warehouse that can handle serious growth.
If you’re running meaningful volume and want a WMS that brings structure instead of chaos, ShipHero is absolutely worth it.

classification.summary: An established ecommerce brand praises ShipHero's WMS for enabling scalable, disciplined fulfillment operations with strong support and is not looking to switch.

content.body:
Easyship is fine for small startups, but once you cross 100+ orders per day, it becomes slow, unreliable, and frustrating to manage.
As order volume increases, the platform struggles badly. Almost every day there’s a new software issue—orders not syncing properly, automation rules failing, or random errors that force you to manually edit shipments one by one. That manual work easily eats up hours daily, which completely defeats the purpose of using a shipping automation tool.
Their automation is unreliable at scale. Rules don’t apply consistently, and you constantly have to double-check and fix things manually.
Chat support is always “available,” but that doesn’t mean effective. Most of the time, support agents don’t fully understand the technical issues. They usually say “we’ll refer this to our tech team”, and then you hear nothing for days, often with no real solution in the end.

classification.summary: FLEXWOLF is frustrated with Easyship's inability to handle high order volumes reliably and is looking for a more scalable shipping automation solution.

content.body:
Very disappointing experience as a long-term tenant.

We are paying over $10,000 per month in rent, yet the level of service we receive is extremely unfriendly and unprofessional. The Los Angeles–based sales representative is very difficult to communicate with and shows little willingness to support existing customers.

What is most unacceptable is that long-term customers are being charged $0.3 per square foot MORE than new customers. We have confirmed that other tenants, working with different sales representatives, were able to receive a $0.3/ft price reduction, while our sales rep refused to offer the same adjustment with no reasonable explanation.

It is hard to understand why loyal customers are treated worse than new ones. For the amount of rent we pay, this level of service and pricing discrimination is simply unacceptable.

I would strongly advise management to review their pricing policies and sales practices, especially regarding how existing customers are treated.
This feels like a penalty for being a loyal customer rather than a benefit.

If I could, no stars for them. And recommend for those who are consider to rent this facility. Think more about it.

classification.summary: Long-term tenant frustrated with unfair pricing discrimination and poor sales support despite paying over $10k/month in rent.

metadata.owner_response:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We're sorry to hear about your frustration and appreciate the opportunity to provide some clarity.

When a fixed-term agreement transitions to a month-to-month arrangement, pricing adjusts based on our MTM rates, which are applied consistently across locations and differ from promotional rates tied to longer-term agreements. Because of this, comparisons between tenants can vary depending on timing, contract terms, and space type.

That said, we take concerns about communication and professionalism seriously, and we regret if your experience did not reflect the level of transparency and service we strive to provide. Your feedback has been shared internally as we continue working to improve.

We value your business and long-term tenancy. If you need further assistance or have questions about your account, our Customer Support team would be happy to help at (800)
338-6369. Thank you again for your feedback-it helps us improve.

content.body: The expectation was integrated prices at shipping with accurate duties and good shipping prices.  Integration seems to be really good!  BUT, the shipping prices are literally more expensive than I can find anywhere and the platform cannot figure out how to accurately represent our duties and taxes.  For example, our product is USMCA compliant and has a beneficial HTS code, which has our US tariff rate at $0, but Easyship is trying to charge our US customers $72 extra.  The additional paperwork and time to sort this out, makes using this platform a complete waste of time.  If you are able to get competitive shipping prices and fix the duties, I'd give this 5 stars for sure!

content.body:
Shipstation has a stranglehold on shipping, so I still use them for some things. But they are exceedingly bad. For example, 19 days ago Shipstation changed international shipping defaults so that I am now covering duties and taxes for international shipments. But, they did not notify me or my team of this setting being defaulted to "on" all the time. The result is that I have accidentally incurred massive costs that were meant to be paid by my customers and that I did not account for. I'm still waiting on invoices to come in from my couriers to see what the total loss is.
I contacted their support and they showed me how to turn this off. But when I asked about why it was set to on for 19 days, and why no one notified me of this critical change to defaults; the best I got was an apology. I wonder how many users are unwittingly incurring these fees. The setting is burried well below the customs declaration in an area with obscure an infrequently used settings, so I suspect that many users are not aware.

classification.summary: Shipstation made a critical change to international shipping defaults without notification, causing the author to accidentally incur significant costs that should have been paid by customers.

content.body:
If given the option, I would immediately discontinue using ShipStation. The platform suffers from persistent bugs and an ineffective support team that consistently fails to resolve reported issues.
In recent experience, even basic functionality—such as real-time synchronization with Shopify—frequently ceases without warning. No alerts or notifications are provided when sync failures occur, forcing users to detect problems reactively, often the following day. Manual intervention is then required to restore synchronization.
Overall, ShipStation delivers a highly unreliable and poorly maintained software solution that falls far below acceptable standards for e-commerce integration tools.

classification.summary: User is actively dissatisfied with ShipStation's unreliable sync functionality and poor support, and is looking to discontinue the service immediately.

content.body:
Coming from traditional self-storage, ReadySpaces has been a game changer. Having power, internet, a break room with a fridge and microwave, a bookable conference room, dumpster access, loading docks, hand carts, and pallet jacks makes a huge difference for us.

All of the amenities are great, but what really makes this place stand out is the manager, Shawn. From the moment we moved in, he’s been incredibly helpful and genuinely invested in keeping everything running smoothly. He’s personable, but also willing to enforce the rules that keep things fair and functional for everyone. You can tell he really cares about the businesses here.

I never feel like I’m bothering him when I need something (which means a lot since I’m usually hesitant to ask for help). Anytime something needs attention — like replacing the water filter or fixing a minor issue — it’s usually handled within a day or two.

If I had one suggestion, it would be to implement a more streamlined and secure system for package deliveries. You can receive packages now, but the process could be improved.

Overall, ReadySpaces has been an excellent move for us — the facilities are great, and the management makes all the difference.

classification.summary: ReadySpaces customer is satisfied with facilities and management but suggests improving their package delivery system.

content.body:
Beware, Beware, Beware!This morning, I added $550 to my ShipStation balance to purchase shipping labels for our orders. Shortly after, the company triple-charged our account, taking an additional $1,100.
I reached out to their support team and provided proof of the unauthorized charges — each transaction clearly lists ShipStation in the description. Now, Shopify is investigating them for these unauthorized withdrawals.
After further review, I discovered that other businesses have also been affected by ShipStation overcharging their accounts.

classification.summary: Business experienced unauthorized triple-charging by ShipStation and is actively seeking alternatives after discovering systemic overcharging issues.

content.body:
If zero stars were possible, Easyship would earn it.
U.S. customs changes are always announced months in advance, yet nothing is ever updated in the platform to handle USMCA compliance or prevent unjustified tariffs. As a result, our fully compliant Canadian products are charged duties anyway — forcing us to absorb part of the cost just to stay fair to our customers.
When a single fulfillment team member couldn’t pass the in-app ID verification, the entire business account was disabled for 24–48 hours. That meant no shipping, no fulfillment — everything on hold.
Customer “support” is another disaster. Every interaction ends with someone who can’t actually resolve issues, and when they do act, it’s without any understanding of how real businesses operate. One rep even “fixed” a carrier error by delaying our pickup three days without telling us — and called that a solution.
Only thing they are good at is giving apologies...
Run! Don’t get involved or subscribe to Easyship. There’s nothing “easy” about it — and it’s certainly not built for serious shippers or fulfillment-driven businesses.

classification.summary: Frustrated ecommerce brand actively seeking to leave Easyship due to poor compliance support, account disruptions, unreliable customer service, and unexpected tariff costs.

content.body:
UPDATE 2nd September 2025: We're leaving our current 3PL because of this garbage app - it continues to sync our non USA orders causing a lot of inventory and reporting issues.
Our 3PL uses ShipHero. Imagine waking up and finding orders cancelled and refunded in Shopify after a member of staff at our 3PL cancelled those orders in ShipHero.
How on earth can ShipHero and an unknown member of staff at our 3PL using it have the authority to refund our Shopify orders? We obviously lost $$$$ in addition to getting customer complaints.
Obviously we'd never authorisation for that.
And the 2nd major issue is that we have multiple warehouse locations, but ShipHero will not sync only the orders at the location we want. It will sync ALL orders at ALL locations even though our 3PL doesn't process orders at those locations. Which brings up all kinds of data protection issues in addition to a messy ShipHero dashboard. How come you can't provide such a basic feature. If we just want our Timbuktu orders syncing, then that's what should happen.
Finally, adjusting an order at a location will amend the inventory at all locations.A highly annoying issue.
If ShipHero can't fix these core issues we'll actually leave our 3PL for another one - it's that bad.
ShipHero has been fast asleep the last4 weeks since our 3PL reported these issues to them - still no response.

classification.summary: Customer is actively leaving their current 3PL due to critical issues with ShipHero's inability to sync orders by location, unauthorized refund capabilities, and poor customer service response.

content.body:
I do not recommend ShipStation. During the trial, the experience started off positive. I was supported and even assigned an account manager. Once the trial ended, that support disappeared. My account manager stopped responding to my emails, my account was locked, and I was prevented from cancelling my subscription despite following their instructions and contacting support.
I attempted multiple times to cancel in any way I could, yet I continued receiving invoices while still unable to access my account. Their support system is convoluted, unhelpful, and failed to resolve the issue.
This is only my experience, but while the trial looked promising and human-centered, I quickly faced access issues, ongoing billing, convoluted support, and a cancellation process driven by automation where I ultimately fell through the cracks.

classification.summary: Customer experienced poor support, account access issues, and billing problems with ShipStation after trial period ended, expressing frustration with the cancellation process and lack of customer service.

content.body:
We’ve been with ReadySpaces Harris for over a year now and have been very happy with both the space and the service. Before moving in, we visited three ReadySpaces locations, and all the managers we met were polite, helpful, and quick to answer any questions. The locations were generally clean, well-maintained, and had all the amenities we were looking for, such as pallet jacks and mail services.

Since moving into the Harris location, we’ve been especially pleased—particularly with Adam, the manager. He’s professional, reliable, and always quick to respond. He’s also added helpful features like a shared printer and additional carts/dollies, and he keeps the facility in great shape.

When we first moved in, we realized we needed more space than expected. Adam was very accommodating and helped us secure an additional unit right away. We also inquired about improving the lighting in our unit, and not long after, the lighting throughout the building was upgraded.

Overall, it’s been a great experience at ReadySpaces Harris, and we truly appreciate the continued support and service from the team here.

content.body:
We have a unit at WareSpace and are enjoying the facility. Our unit is suited to what we need, and we were able to get shelving moved for us (I think there was a small charge). We liked that there are units of various sizes that make it attainable to have a warehouse space at a reasonable price. We appreciate the loading docks for loading up for daily jobs.

The community space is nice and always pristinely clean. There is a kitchen with plenty of tables plus refrigerator (with filtered water and ice), Keurig (with free K-cups to make drinks), sink, foosball table, and more. There are conference rooms that can be rented for free when they are available. We’ve never had a conflict getting a conference room when needed. Restrooms are accessible and clean.

The site manager, Georgiana, is incredibly friendly and helpful, always ready to answer questions with a smile on her face. She brings packages to units, as needed, which is very nice and helpful.

The only issue is that parking can be tight sometimes, though I’ve really only had an issue once. Others on my team have had more difficulty from time to time.

This has been great for the needs of our team, and we are very happy with our choice!

classification.summary: A satisfied WareSpace customer in the Chicago area is praising their facility, management, and amenities while noting only minor parking issues.

content.body:
### Warning to Future Users: Proceed with Caution – Easyship Failed My Business
**Rating: ★☆☆☆☆ (1/5)**  If I could give 0 stars, I would.
I’m writing this review as a warning to other Shopify store owners who may be considering Easyship. My experience has been frustrating and costly—and I want to make sure others fully understand what they’re signing up for.
**1. Nonexistent Technical Support**When I encountered issues with shipments, I received nothing but canned apologies and zero real help. There is no technical support when you actually need it. Reaching someone who can resolve issues in a timely manner is nearly impossible. This is unacceptable for a platform that claims to support e-commerce businesses.
**2. Unreliable Service = Disrupted Operations**Shipping is the backbone of any online store, and with Easyship, my operations were disrupted repeatedly. I had shipments pending with no clarity, and no one followed up to resolve the issues. For a service that markets itself as a logistics solution, this is a serious failure.
**3. No Accountability or Action**I received plenty of apologies, but apologies don’t fix broken processes. I needed action, and it never came. My business cannot afford delays and miscommunication when it comes to order fulfillment.
**4. Refund and Cancellation Issues**I’ve requested a full cancellation of my annual subscription and a refund—not just for the subscription, but also for the cancelled shipments that are still pending. I expect this to be processed without further delay. I’ve lost both time and money due to this platform.
---
### Final Word: I Do Not Recommend
If you’re serious about your business and need a dependable shipping solution with actual support, **look elsewhere**. Easyship failed to deliver on even the most basic expectations. I’m not happy, and I’m sharing this review so other entrepreneurs don’t fall into the same trap.
After my disappointing experience with Easyship, I’ve made the switch to NetParcel, a Canadian-based shipping platform.  So far, installation has been seamless, and everything is based out of Shopify.  No need to leave Shopify to create labels.

classification.summary: User is actively switching away from Easyship due to poor technical support, unreliable shipping operations, and lack of accountability, and has already moved to NetParcel.

content.body:
Easyship is so close. It gets a lot right. The shipping rules system is powerful, integration with Shopify is good, the API and Webhooks are solid, order merge is awesome, analytics are insightful, and DDP support is great.
It lacks in a few key areas though that have been unaddressed after reaching out many times:- FedEx International shipping does not support EDI when using their shipping account, we've been waiting 6+ months for this and had been told it would be done "soon"- FedEx International shipping (using Easyshi's account) is missing from some key markets like Germany and Australia for no clear reason. Support had no idea and didn't seem to follow up with FedEx- Flat Export does not support door to door tracking when available like its direct competitor (Pirate Ship: Simple Export) despite using the same Asendia Service
These issues make dealing with international shipping really challenging, which is the sole reason we switched to Easyship.

classification.summary: Customer switched to Easyship for international shipping but is frustrated with unresolved FedEx EDI support, limited market coverage, and inferior tracking features compared to competitors.

content.body: ABSOLUTELY THE WORST SERVICE! What a scam! We first signed up for a free trial to see how it could help us with our growing e-commerce business. We had a lot of questions and the rep we were assigned was on vacation. He came back right when our free trial was ending. We got on an onboarding call and he refused to extend our free trial so that we could give the platform a try. We even asked for just a couple of days but we were told no and we would need to sign up or use a new email address. We decided to use a new email and do a new free-trial. We got this peppy representative names Amanda Bellow. She seemed helpful and guided us through the platform. We began trying to ship and encountered a series of issues (our account constantly disconnected from the ship connect and we couldn't batch print, packing slips were not customizable, and orders would not load from shopify) We got back on a call with Amanda (with great difficulty because this young girl is so busy) and she told us to upgrade to a premium plan so that we could access customizable packing slips. She noted all the other issues and said someone would get back to us. We'll spare you the torture of all the back and forth we went through with their AI and completely ineffective email support but bottom line we were told the solution was to uninstall and reinstall ship connect ever time we want to ship (amazing! What a great solution for a small business that is growing! We have so much extra time on our hands) as for the packing slips we never got any assistance. Regarding our Shopify orders not coming through, we were told to reconnect our store every time and give it a few hours for our orders to populate or recreate them directly in ship station (basically adding manual work to all the features that are supposed to be automated). We tried downgrading our plan since we couldn't use their packing slip feature and it wouldn't let us. We tried to reach their support team - no answer. We contacted Amanda who said she was so busy onboarding new customers she wasn't able to help but gave us a number to call. Around that time we started having issues with our account not connecting to our shipping carrier rates. We contacted support and called that number. George told us there was nothing he could do. It was a UPS problem. We explained that our negotiated rates showed up perfectly well on Shopify and UPS but he insisted we needed to call UPS. We asked if he could give us our next billing date because we were considering cancelling. He told us we could just cancel online and clearly, by his tone and lack of engagement this guy couldn't have cared any less about providing any further help. We called UPS (even though we had of course done that already and UPS confirmed that all of our rates are active and the issue is coming from ship station) We called back Ship station and were told they simply cannot help us when it comes to carrier rates (but they do charge $30/month for this service) Honestly, this company is absolute BS when it comes to their clunky technology and horrible customer service. And they clearly have 0 understanding when it comes to the need of small businesses. Clearly their focus is on getting people to sign up since I doubt they can keep their customers long term. Save yourself a headache. If you don't ship with dry ice, check out Pirate Ship or Shopify direct. If you ship with Dry Ice, your best bet might be to use your carrier directly even though, we know, it can be a pain. At least you will be able to ship. DO NOT WASTE YOUR TIME with this S*%!TSTATION!

classification.summary: Small e-commerce business frustrated with platform's technical issues, poor support responsiveness, and inability to customize packing slips, actively seeking alternatives after failed onboarding experience.

content.body:
I have been with ShipStation since 2017 and had a great experience in the past. However, after canceling my subscription for two months (November 2024), trying to restart it in March 2025 has been nothing but a nightmare.
Previously, I would have highly recommended ShipStation to anyone. The chat support was phenomenal—any issue I had was resolved within minutes. It was easily one of my favorite business programs I used that made my life easier and never had an issue. Unfortunately, in 2025, that level of support is nonexistent.
I've encountered problem after problem:
-Unable to log in-Not receiving emails for code confirmations, password resets, or account activation-Unable to reconnect my store-Configuration issues with the app-Unable to add payment to my account
The amount of time I’ve wasted trying to set everything up again has been frustrating and exhausting. ShipStation has clearly made changes, and not for the better. I can no longer recommend this app to any business owner and have since switched to a new company.

classification.summary: A former ShipStation user is venting about severe service degradation and support quality decline after attempting to restart their subscription, and has already switched to a competitor.

author.name: Jon Brown
author.company: Portal Warehousing | Small Warehouse & Logistics Space in Brooklyn

content.body:
I’ll start this review with a story.

I had just moved into my unit at Portal—barely a week in—when I had a major delivery scheduled. Thirteen pallets were set to arrive at 2 PM, and I had my team scheduled to show up at 1 PM to unload them. Everything was planned perfectly… until the truck decided to show up at 10 AM instead.

Cue the panic. I was about 30 minutes away, and my workers weren’t set to arrive for another three hours. Then I got a call from Jeff, the Manager, letting me know about the early arrival. I explained the situation, expecting to have to rush over and figure it out. Instead, Jeff simply said, “Don’t worry about it—I’ll handle it.”

By the time I arrived at 11 AM, the pallets were already moved, neatly placed, and all I had to do was unload them into my unit. Jeff had taken care of it himself. That’s the kind of service and support you get at Portal.

Beyond just professionalism, Jeff and the entire team truly go above and beyond to make sure your day-to-day operations run smoothly. Whether it’s their depth of knowledge, willingness to collaborate, or just the welcoming atmosphere, it’s clear they understand the needs of entrepreneurs.

The facility itself is top-notch—clean, modern, and well-designed. The lounge area feels like a WeWork, providing a great space to network or get work done. Before this, I was juggling three different storage units, and switching to Portal has been a game-changer.

If you’re looking for a space that supports your business and genuinely cares about your success, this is it.

classification.summary: A satisfied Portal (co-warehousing facility) customer praises exceptional service, responsive management, and modern facilities that improved their operations compared to juggling multiple storage units.

content.body:
We've had a mixed experience with Shipmonk as a fulfillment service. While there are some positives, there have been significant challenges that affected our operations.
* International Order Shipping: We only achieved reliable international shipping after partnering with Passport, which revealed weaknesses in our initial approach.
* Shipping from the UK to Europe: Relying on Royal Mail instead of globally recognized carriers like DHL or DPD caused major delays. Other fulfillment centers provided faster and more cost-effective solutions.
* Customer Reputation Impact: Shipping delays led to an influx of negative reviews, hurting our brand. It took a lot of effort to regain customer trust.
* Claim Filing Limitations: Unlike other warehouses, Shipmonk does not offer an intuitive claim filing process, meaning we had to absorb losses when issues arose.
* Lost Items During Receiving: Items marked as "not seen" had no recourse for claims, adding to financial losses.
* Service Discrepancy: While customer service has been helpful, the warehouse operations and contract terms fall short compared to other providers.
Overall, Shipmonk shows potential, but its logistics and fulfillment processes still need greater reliability and flexibility. We've seen significant improvements over the past year and remain hopeful for continued progress in these areas. Throughout our experience, the customer service team has been outstanding—special thanks to Cassandra for her exceptional support. However, some operational challenges are clearly beyond her control.

classification.summary: Ecommerce brand frustrated with ShipMonk's unreliable international shipping, poor claim processes, and operational inconsistencies, despite positive customer service, and actively seeking better fulfillment alternatives.

content.body:
I’ve been using ShipBob for over a year now, and honestly, I’ve had nothing but problems. I don’t like writing negative reviews, but I feel like this one is necessary.
The biggest issue for me has been their support team. I’d open a ticket about an issue, they’d tell me it’s fixed, and then the same thing would happen again a few days later. For example, my TikTok Shop orders weren’t syncing properly, and there were constant SKU problems. This went on for over a month before it was finally resolved. Then, once that got sorted, shipping updates stopped syncing to TikTok, so I had to manually update them myself. It’s just one thing after another.
On top of that, I was told shipping costs would be between $7–$9, but I’ve been charged way more than that multiple times. And today, I had a customer receive the wrong color product entirely! It’s always these little things that add up, and I’m beyond frustrated.
After I finish this bulk order, I’m switching to another shipping company. ShipBob charges way too much for the level of service they provide. I really hope they can figure things out for other customers, but I’m done.
I also forgot to mention i always get duty fees in the mail and packages  always get lost when i ship them to my models.

classification.summary: Customer is actively leaving ShipBob due to persistent support failures, billing overages, fulfillment errors, and integration issues, and is seeking an alternative shipping/fulfillment provider.

I like the platform - but very frustrating dealing with them.

content.body:
EDIT: Easyship did get in contact with me and refunded the overcharges that were assessed over the prior month for me to my satisfaction. We have not seen more UPS multi-package overcharges so they may have corrected the problem with USP. We have also been trying to avoid using UPS for multi-package when possible because of this but we will give it a try again in the future. I am leaving my initial review for the time being so people see the whole picture. The only way that the problem received attention to resolve the issue was with the review below. Hopefully they will act faster with application problems faster in the future.
ORIGINAL REVIEW: This APP was working pretty well for well over a year and then started sending us UPS overcharges weekly for multi package shipments. Some times more than double what the APP had quoted us. This caused us to lose money on a number of orders and when we contacted Easyship they insisted that it was not their responsibility and requested detailed pictures of packages that had left our building weeks before in oder to file a claim. They then wanted us to recreate the packages and send them pictures wasting time and materials on our end. They say that it is up to the courier to have the final say. Even when they can check the system and see that we had shipped two packages and printed two labels they allow UPS to come back and bill for four packages. There is a serious communication issue between this platform and the UPS API and when pressing the chat agent to speak with someone in management my request fell on deaf ears. I am sure that I can not be the only customer having this issue so I would assume that Easyship has no real interest in resolving the problem and is simply going to rely on the fine print in their agreement so point the finger at UPS and continue to allow them to bill in excess of the services rendered. In all honesty you should keep shopping for a shipping APP and skip this one entirely. They have somehow managed to not allow Google reviews of their company. That alone tells you something.

classification.summary: Customer experienced repeated UPS overcharging issues through Easyship's shipping platform and encountered poor customer service when trying to resolve billing discrepancies, though the issue was eventually partially resolved after leaving a negative review.

content.body:
Absolutely, unequivocally a 0 star experience.  We are a former customer who used Shipstation for years through 2022, and attempted to reinitiate service 11/2024 with no success and ABSOLUTELY no assistance from Shipstation Support.
I would be extremely confident in stating that there is no service. "Chat" (unless you have their highest tiered service) is an unhelpful AI that simply reroutes you to their support pages; thereafter, when one of their "support" members initiates the chat, it is often in the middle of the night when we are asleep in the US.  If you do not respond right away, they close out your ticket.  For the 3 representatives from which I attempted to get any kind of help reinitiating my account and Shopify store, they ALL rerouted me to their support pages, even when I gave them screenshots of the error (broken token from my store) and informed each new representative of all futile past attempts at assistance.
We are a former customer of Shipstation, and they used to have superior service.  It is unfortunate to say that no longer exists, and I cannot recommend anyone to their service.  I canceled my unused - yet twice billed - account today.  I have attempted to get my account working since late November 2024 to no avail.  This is truly a shame and I'd say stay far, far, far away.

classification.summary: A former Shipstation customer is actively switching away due to poor support, technical issues, and billing problems after attempting to reinitiate service in late 2024.

content.body:
I've used Easyship (and their affiliated warehouse for inventory management and fulfilment) for a few years now and overall, it's a good combination as a small business owner. They drastically reduce the time spent on fulfilment so that I can focus on other parts of the business.
While they offer favourable rates on most couriers due to their volume, if you're often using your local postal services, this will cost quite a bit more than doing it yourself because they don't offer discounts, and Easyship's margins will add to that cost. But for the time saved, plus the ability to offer my customers 3 shipping options each time (cheapest, fastest and best value), it's a no-brainer for me to continue using this service.
There have been hiccups along the way but most of the time (as I'd expect from all companies who offer global shipping), their support is quick and efficient.

classification.summary: Happy Easyship customer noting higher costs for local postal services but satisfied with time savings and shipping options offered.

content.body: I worked late last night until about 12 am and signed up for a trial account with them before going to sleep. During registration, I filled in my details, including our business address in Australia and my mobile number. Their team called just a few minutes after I signed up around midnight, which made my dog bark loudly and woke up my baby, who cried for hours. It was ridiculous since they didn’t even check the time zone before calling.

classification.summary: Customer is dissatisfied with Saltbox's aggressive midnight outreach call that violated timezone awareness and caused personal disruption.

content.body: Manas instrumental to making seamless integration!

content.body: Shipstation support is a joke. They are foolish, ignorant, and will waste your time with multiple basic responses that are as useless as asking if the computer is turned on... IYKYK. Unfortunately this is NOT a 1 time occurrence, it is the norm. For example, I submitted a bug report where the packing slip is literally printing the words "[notes from buyer]" instead of the actual notes from buyer field contents for random orders. I submitted PICTURES of an actual printed packing slip of what it looks like when it generates correctly, and another picture of what it looks like with incorrect generation. I pay extra for an upgraded plan to be able to customize my packing slips. I created this packing slip about 8+ YEARS ago. Shipstations response? Their "answer" is that they do not help with HTML. Uuuuuhm NO this is NOT an HTML issue whatsoever, and they'd know this if they looked at my example pictures even just once. JUST ONCE. LOOK. AT. THE. PICTURES! If I did the HTML wrong I would not have an example of a correct packing slip generation, right? This is just ONE packing slip template that's affected, and it's random orders. But it'll be a miracle if I have to waste less than 10 emails just to get them to even check out the bug. The crazy thing is that back in the day, around 10+ years ago an English speaking USA based support rep used to pick up the phone and call you. Imagine that!

classification.summary: Customer is frustrated with ShipStation's poor support quality and inability to resolve a recurring packing slip template bug despite providing detailed documentation.

content.body: My honest opinion. I used this app at first because it seems easier and more reliable. it was working fine with a few issues here and there. when you ship hazardous items it doesn't mention it on the label at all so you can have problems without realizing(Its my first time so I had no idea), and when you ship international using certain carriers it doesn't give you the full list of the things you need or that are okay. for example it doesn't tell you that FedEx needs you to buy their boxes only, that they will use the wrong label size and weight you had for your previous box and put it on the new one. result is you pay insane custom fees for the wrong label when you didn't do anything wrong :) beware. the support was decent. sometimes you get someone useful sometimes not. most of the time I sent emails and didn't get answers back. the only time they worked hard was to tell me to change my review if they fixed the issue which is why I'm writing this.Lauretta. S.Vaas helped me with my FedEx issue and refund therefore I'm giving this review 4 stars  just because of her. for the general experience i would give 2 stars at best.

classification.summary: User experienced issues with hazardous/international shipping label accuracy and support responsiveness, resulting in unexpected customs fees, though they credit individual support staff member with preventing a lower rating.

content.body:
We’ve been working with ShipMonk for over half a year now and their commitment to customer service and reliability has truly impressed us. During the recent Canada Post strike—an incredibly challenging time for our business—ShipMonk went above and beyond to ensure our operations continued without disruption. They seamlessly transitioned our shipments to FedEx Ground the same day, giving us peace of mind and ensuring our customers weren’t left waiting.
The onboarding process with ShipMonk was challenging, but it's understandable, given the breadth of industries they serve. They’ve designed their systems to cater to various needs, and their effort to ensure everything works smoothly is worth it.
What stands out most is their accommodating nature and their support for our team. ShipMonk consistently demonstrates a willingness to adapt and go the extra mile, which is rare in logistics. We truly enjoy working with them.
If you’re looking for a partner that truly prioritizes your success, ShipMonk is a fantastic choice. Thank you for your incredible service during this busy and challenging season—we genuinely appreciate the difference you’ve made for our business!

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipMonk customer in Canada is praising their service and reliability, particularly their response to a Canada Post strike, with no indication of interest in switching providers.

content.body: I used ShipMonk as my 3PL for a silver jewelry company for almost 2 years... To start with, the onboarding was really frustrating and took WAY longer than the expectation that was set. Then we spent weeks changing the processes because their fulfillment team could not comprehend the proper way to package our simple items in their boxes correctly, despite documents and videos being provided. We stuck with it because we figured it was an "investment" that would pay off once we got it right. So we did stick with it, until I had virtually no other choice than to cancel them and get my inventory back due to the amount of my items that went "missing" while in their possession. My customers would regularly receive empty boxes, even though our in-house team packaged them and sealed them. This caused a lot of issues and multiple poor reviews. AND, when we finally received all of our remaining inventory back, not only was everything extremely dusty, but DOZENS of items were missing from the expected line sheet that they provided. Unfortunately, I think their staff is ruining what could be a great service, because the platform was excellent. But they have crooked staff in the fulfillment department and some lazy/uninterested staff in their customer service department. If left 2 stars because I have to give them credit for their great Shopify integration and really user friendly dashboard and because, once we finally got the process down, the fulfillment was working well. But, until they get rid of their bad apples, prevent the dust from piling up on the products in the warehouse, and hire some customer service reps that actually care, they will continue to get poor feedback.

classification.summary: A jewelry company switched away from ShipMonk after nearly 2 years due to critical fulfillment failures including missing inventory, empty boxes, poor onboarding, and unhelpful customer service, and is now seeking a reliable 3PL alternative.

content.body:
This is the only real usable shipping software for companies. There are so many options for both domestic but most importantly international.
Many shipping software don't have Fedex as an option, you have to add your own account. Fedex has a new shipping option, International connect plus that is so affordable and quick. (Fedex only allows this if you have your own account if you ship 30k worth of packages a year.) None of my customers say that there are additional brokerage fees yet.
The userface is really easy to use. You can do bulk edits very easily. You can merge same destination shipments (shopify does not let you do that, you need an app). There is a feature that lets you automatically add insurance. You can create a manifest. You can track the packages easily and ask for a refund for the label and insurance claims, and when you process the labels the custom forms populate with the shipping labels.
On my computer the software lags extensively, and there are printer issues that emerged, as in the software would not populate shipping labels and if it did it would not be formatted correctly. I was told to use a different browser and that did fix the printer issue. Dhl is much cheaper on shopify, sometimes as much as $20 and shopify offers free DHL pickup with the purchase of the label.
The really cool feature is what your customers see at checkout. They will see 3 options, cheap, middle, and quickest/most expensive. Then, your international customers will also see what they will pay in taxes and duties. That feature was on my site for the first 2 years. Then, it wasnt. I contacted easyship and they said it was shopify, I contacted shopify and they said it was easyship. I was on a grandfathered plan for both of them and after the hassle and 2 years, i decided to upgrade both plans and that still did not solve it.
The onboarding process was great. Mine was years ago so not sure how it is now.
This is a great software, there are issues and sometimes they are fixed and sometimes they are not. But compared to other shipping software this is really great.

classification.summary: User praises easyship's shipping software capabilities and international options but expresses frustration with technical issues (lag, printer problems) and a disappeared feature that support couldn't resolve across multiple plan upgrades.

content.body:
I have  been working with and for a company that has been utilizing this ReadyStorage work / storage / venue location for quite some time. That said I personally and for quite some time prior to Trevor taking over manager duties at this location I wasn't the biggest fan of this ReadyStorage location.

I don't remember off hand how long it's been since Trevor took the reigns as  Manager of the ReadySpaces Storage Facility at 1185 Campbell, but from day one and  little by little he's been able to take this old dilapidated monster maze of a building  brought it back from the coffin. I work on site or pickup from the facility at least a few times a month so I've def noticed and recognize the work that Trevor has put into the spot. (From Digital App Based Locks to new Carpets in the conference room, new Coffee machines  in the breakroom, are all Trevor's fault.) Prior to Trevor it felt like the place had no soul, and now has more of a community feel. I don't life in the south bay, however if i did I'd look to Readystorage and of course Trevor for all my storage needs.

classification.summary: A satisfied customer praises the management improvements at ReadyStorage under Trevor's leadership, noting the facility's transformation from poor conditions to a community-oriented workspace.

content.body: WORST customer service. Shipstation worked great till they switched to Parcel Guard. They ended up costing us thousands of dollars in damages for one shipment. We have double automatic insurance rules on our account, but what they don't advertise is that if you ship more than one package in a shipment, they automatically turn off your insurance. Been back and forth with customer service for months over the issue. Cancelled our account because of how much money we lost. Said they were going to give us a refund, went silent for weeks, stopped responding to our emails then a new support person popped back in after we cancelled and said sorry to see you go and no refund.

classification.summary: Customer switched from Shipstation to Parcel Guard and lost thousands in uncompensated shipping damages due to undisclosed insurance policy changes and poor customer service, leading them to cancel and seek alternatives.

content.body: Terrible integration with Shopify.- If an order is edited (products change, address updated, notes added etc) that edit in Shopify does not flow over to Shipstation. So as a small business owner I need to make the edits twice.- I have two different warehouses and Shopifys split shipping is not supported as an integration with Shipstation.- I have an app on Shopify that makes beautiful, branded packing slips and invoices. But these do not travel over to Shipstation so my packages have this totally mediocre packing slip thats not on-brand for my upscale company.- I sell wholesale on Faire and while Faire and Shopify integrate beautifully when the orders flow through to Shipstation they are all screwed up with testers not registering properly and discounts showing up in a very confusing way. So every time I get a wholesale order through Faire I need to manually email them the packing slip so they can pick and fill correctly.I love my warehouse but wish they did not need to use Shipstation. I feel Shipstation does not care one bit about the business using Shopify, only about the warehouses.

classification.summary: Small business owner frustrated with Shipstation's poor Shopify integration, lack of support for split shipping, and inability to use branded packing slips, requiring manual workarounds for wholesale orders.

content.body: The Shipmonk team is lazy, messy, sloppy and corrupt. They do not use FIFO for picking and packing inventory. They regularly lose goods. Their packing is outrageously sloppy, they have regular billing errors, Their inventory data is always wrong, they will never give you credit if they lose your inventory and worst of all they make it almost impossible to leave. In order to terminate a relationship with them you need to hire them to pick and pack all your goods. They make this experience take a very very very long time and during the process they charge you minimum fees, storage fees and also make other billing errors. If you don't overpay based on their billing errors they stop the work to get your goods out.  I've been trying to get my inventory back from them for over 6 months, and they are making it next to impossible.  STAY AWAY FROM THIS CROOKED COMPANY!

classification.summary: A dissatisfied ShipMonk customer seeking to switch providers due to poor inventory management, billing errors, lost goods, and difficulty exiting their contract.

content.body:
This review is about Trevor new the general manager of their facility and readyspaces.

First, why I chose readyspaces. It has warehouse facilties of different sizes but you also have very nice confernce rooms, common kitchen, wifi fir everyone even in the warehouse, mail in and mail out in secure area, video security, two docks, different entry gates, common fork and pallet lifting machines etc......bottom line you get both office and warehosue without paying much..if you don't need big office area and your business does require more inventory space then it has all and that's pretty unique. Now, one advantage when you don't know about the right size of the warehosue as business grows, you can always scale up or scale down based on what's needed.
Readyspaces no wonder is one one for fastest growing companies in a space whoch was already crowded because it is solving problems for many small busineses.

Now about Trevor the new general manager. He has given a soul to this body. He has all the right traits of a great leader who can manage complex operations of a business such as readyspaces. He is there all the time, very reachable and can be reached anytime through any methods..say email, call, in person..this is innstark contrast to the previous one who was very hard to find...I have seen a lot of renovations after he has joined. He has been a positive chnage agent for this place. Restrooms have been upgraded, conference rooms have been remodeled, floor, mats, new entry mechnism via phone...on n on...Many of his qualities , such actively listening to customers and solving their problems proactively, do anything to make customers happy, ( one such example..when one customer came to drop my product, non of the employees or myself was in office)..he called me and got the device and kept in his office...), he is getting investments for the place, and finally..the word soul or a great soul that enlightens the entire place with his hardwork, active engagements, proactive actions and customer collaborations..

classification.summary: A satisfied customer praises ReadySpaces' flexible warehouse and office solutions and highlights the positive leadership impact of their new general manager Trevor who has improved operations and customer service.

One of the most impressive aspects of ShipHero is its flexibility. Our warehouse environment is complex, with a wide range of products, storage configurations, and unique processes. content.body:
Don't often write reviews but feel an obligation to warn other business owners. Shipbob were very good during the sales process but as soon as we started operating they were useless. We are a UK based business and intended to use shipbob to fulfil EU orders (post brexit).
However, despite following shipbob's UK to EU stock transfer process, our shipments were always fraught with crossborder issues. Our stock transfers were always at least 4 weeks late due to incompetency on shipbobs end. It seemed like shipbob had never onboarded a new customer before, crucial information which cost us thousands of pounds was missed and all the problem solving had to be done from our end due to shipbob often not responding to us or couriers.
We used the service for three months and do not recommend for UK businesses looking to oursource their international fulfilment

classification.summary: UK-based ecommerce business is actively seeking alternatives after experiencing severe service failures, delayed stock transfers, and poor communication with ShipBob during international fulfillment operations.

content.body:
They overcharged me on 6 packages from USA, to USA, all of them weighed less then 5lbs and dimensions small, they overcharged by 550$!!!!!! If my store would have been live and i had 100 orders that they they would have ACH charged me probably over 10k without me being able to do anything about it.
And thats not the worst of it, that was for 2 days guaranteed shipbob express delivery, arrived 8-10 days later.
This was resolved 14 days later, and money credited to my shipbob account.
Not only that but they claim the reason for it was that the dimensions of my products were wrong, well i have an email sent from me 2 weeks before telling them to fix the dimensions of some smaller products, and they said thanks we will fix it, confimed it, but apparently never did.
Why is that ? Because 99% of theire customer service is sitting in a call center in INDIA, and they cant help with jack, they just forward internally, and then you will get another message from a USA based agent that takes theire sweet time because they probably manage like a gazillion accounts.
Not only that i have no gone over to a different 3PL, moved everything delayed my launch lost thousands of dollars due to ShipBob and they have the audacity to charge me a PICK FEE of 1100+$ for shipping out my little invetory i had in there that they charged 360$ for shipping btw and they charged me PICK fee for individual SKUs when i allready had my SKUs boxed up and separated all they needed to do was slap on labels.
I was in here reading reviews before i chose shipbob and i was convinced they were good, so im hoping this post can act as a warning for others like me. I chose to go with Fulfyld instead. DYOR.

classification.summary: Customer experienced significant overcharges, poor customer service, and slow delivery with ShipBob, has already switched to Fulfyld, and is warning others about the problems.
