content.body:
This app is a game changer for us.  We sell on various channels including Etsy, Amazon, Shopify and Ebay.  ShipStation pulls in all of our open orders on all of these channels and enables us to manage them in one place.  Unfortunately, not all of the order details can be imported from Amazon (this seems to be an issue from Amazon's end) so we continue to print out our orders from each channel and then cross-reference them with the open orders in ShipStation.
If they could successfully get all the order details pulled in from all of our channels and then provide formatting for us to print the orders including any customizations and customer notes from right within ShipStation I would rate them 5+ stars.  As it is, the app still saves us time and money and pays for itself several times over.  Their support team is also great to work with - very responsive.

classification.summary: Multi-channel seller using ShipStation appreciates consolidated order management but wants better Amazon integration and improved printing/customization features.

content.body:
We started with shipstation about one year ago and we had an interesting dilemma. We are located in Canada, most of our customers are in the United States and shipping with Canada Post is expensive (as compared to USPS).
So we needed a solution where we could ship from our office in Canada (Vancouver Area) through Canada Post & DHL for our USA/Int Express. We also needed a way where we could ship with USPS from the post office across the border. The actual shipping from the USPS office across the border was a task in itself but we figured a great way to do it and reach out to me if you want more info on that.
The problem was I could not figure out how to have two different countries to ship from with Canada Post/DHL/USPS in the mix. I am sure there must be a ton of solutions but the only one that was easy was Goshippo. I really liked goshippo and will recommend it to anyone but it doesnt have the automation and rules that shipstation has. I also tried shipping easy and Ordoro - Both great tools that had automation and easy built into it but couldnt duplicate the total ease of shipstation and the origin shipping as offered by goshippo.
Well the answer is that with everything the easiest way was to maintain two shipstation accounts. The orders still feed into both easily and I can rule and automate the vast majority of the functions. The batch process works well and it was relatively easy to train our shipping team on the process.
In conclusion, I give 5 stars for the quality of the product and the ease of automation. I would still like to see the tool allow for origin shipping that I need but for the price and the small inconvenience it really isnt that bad. I guess I could always use a more enterprise version of another app but we know that those developer fees can add up fast!

classification.summary: Canadian ecommerce brand found a workaround for multi-country shipping limitations by maintaining dual ShipStation accounts, but wishes for better origin shipping flexibility.

content.body:
We previously used ShipHero with Magento EE, but recently moved to Shopify Plus. We are now managing several Shopify Plus stores with ShipHero and it works like a dream.
Some of the best things about ShipHero are:
- iPad based, no more paper everywhere.- Full control with bin locations and overstock.- Fast and accurate picking process. We use carts where we pick into several totes for each picking session, and barcodes are used to verify.- Fast packing process with another round of verification by scanning barcodes or confirming manually.- Returns.- Cycle counting.- Statistics that greatly help when creating purchase orders. You can set re-order levels and amounts, saving you an incredible amount of time. ShipHero tells you how much you sold in the last 7/30/60/90/180/365 days.- Lots of useful reports that seems to get better all the time.- Amazing support!
If you ship anything above 50 orders per day and/or have lots of SKUs with the need for bin and overstock bin locations, the monthly cost is definitely worth it! Using this vs the standard Shopify shipping workflow is like day and night. We had another WMS before ShipHero aimed at larger retailers, so we have tried others, but we are getting faster and more efficient packing and picking with ShipHero.
Conclusion: Highly recommended!

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipHero user is praising their WMS solution and recommending it to others, not seeking alternative fulfillment services.

content.body:
I'm an ex-Shipwire customer, we used them for 6 months. They started out great, my sales rep lured me in with personal attention and quick responses. Sure their prices were high, but their website was well put together and integrated easily with Shopify. Things ran relatively smoothly till they shipped out our first order. We had shipped them marketing inserts, they didn't include them with any of our first 500 orders. So I contacted customer support to fix the issue and they refused to fix it, they also stopped talking to me. So I emailed my old sales rep, he stopped reading my emails. So I guessed the CEO's email and told him how poorly I was treated. He never responded but someone did get back to me eventually. From there on it was nothing but a fight to fix any issue. Their customer service team is garbage.
All of this to say that Fulfillrite has been a breath of fresh air. Their website feels a little older in the graphic design department, but it works great. Their customer service is great. Joe spent time on the phone explaining the shipping process and even helped us by finding a new freight forwarding partner.
Ok so thats all cool, but what about pricing, compared to Shipwire, Fulfillrites prices are amazing. They work with you to find the best prices, packaging and speed for your product. I'll update this if they start screwing things up, but I really doubt they will. We're looking forward to having a great long term partner.

content.body:
If you're looking for a solution that accurately keeps your inventory, allows fast order processing, makes shipping a seamless process you're looking at the right partner.  At www.ThreeBirdNest.com we are a growing online boutique and sell women's fashion apparel and accessories. We were utilizing several different solutions which created many points for errors and lag times on order processing.
Since we have moved over to Ship Hero we ship orders same day, have accurate inventory counts, every item has a location and it's an EASY to use system.
The team is amazing to work with, great support especially in the on boarding process which for us was a lot since we were moving over several different programs into one.
There is way less manual work involved in order processing and we have one place to check for shipments, inventory, processing and orders. Couldn't be happier to give them 5 stars. It really has been a business MUST have for us!

classification.summary: Three Bird Nest is satisfied with their current fulfillment solution (Ship Hero) after switching from multiple disparate systems, highlighting improvements in inventory accuracy, order processing speed, and operational efficiency.

content.body: We were looking at Shipwire for quite some time.  I've had a friend use them having a bad experience, and then just reading the reviews really turned me off.  So when I was ready to make the jump and get a 3rd party fulfillment going, I sought out other options just to see.  I came across Fulfillrite on this Shopify apps center and the reviews looked really good.  Although still a little concerned about the technology integration, backend and pricing, we went ahead and started.  BTW...Joe is awesome to work with, helped with every step of the way to get started and made me feel very comfortable to actually go through with using them (which frankly is very easy too).  Long story short, it's turned out awesome, even exceeding my expectations.  Integration is seamless---their backend works great and lets us monitor and edit things on the fly and manage everything accordingly---customer service is superior, Joe is always extremely helpful---they actually cater to small business incredibly well, meaning you don't need a crazy amount of orders for it to work---pricing is very reasonable and it scales with volume---and they have perfect shipping options for lightweight products which we sell---in a lot of orders so far we've had not one problem.  If you're on the fence I highly recommend them and encourage to make the jump too.  Personally, I had a extensive trip to Mexico that pushed me to get the fulfillment finally started, but in hindsight I should of done it much earlier.  I certainly see Fulfillrite as a long term partner for us.

classification.summary: Customer is sharing a positive review of their experience switching to Fulfillrite for 3PL fulfillment after researching alternatives like Shipwire.

content.body: We have a very unique business and have explored options that would cost us hundreds of thousands of dollars. But the amazing team at Ship Hero helped us tweak a couple of things and we now have a picking & packing solution that is faster than any machine solution we've been presented and it's completely scalable with our rapid growth. On top of that we're probably only using 30% of the true capability! I would highly recommend Ship Hero to any business needing stock, inventory or shipping management!

content.body:
ShipHero has been a real blessing to our operation and has proven itself to be a real solution to a number of time-consuming issues we've had.
The fundamentals of our warehousing process are:-picking orders from about 100 SKUs-determining the most efficient shipping method-packaging the order
ShipHero was likely not designed for our particular needs, but we found it to be highly adaptable and the customer service was truly top notch. We realized just the other day that it was the first day since we had implemented ShipHero that we had not been in contact with their engineers at some point. This level of customer service is what makes a SaaS product great.
Don't misunderstand this statement: features of ShipHero were behaving exactly as intended. We simply had fairly technical questions about how to adapt ShipHero to our needs and the staff were more than happy to help.
I even had one incident where our company was ready to pay ShipHero engineers to develop a custom modification for us. I had a nice screenshare with Aaron Rubin where he pointed out, in an extremely polite and helpful way, that ShipHero was already solving our issue and no development would be needed.
When I first started looking at developing inventory and restocking strategies for our company, I noted the difficulty in tracking changes in inventory due to our 5 different sales channels. ShipHero immediately resolved this issue by giving us the ability to process fulfillment (and thus inventory) for all of our sales channels.
It helped us wrangle out-of-control international shipping losses before they became a serious issue for our finances.
It increased our fulfillment accuracy to the theoretical maximum level (100% accuracy on the human side) by supporting barcode scans and simplifying the flow of decisions for our shipping employees.
ShipHero was designed with Lean best practices in mind. We now have a shipping process that features a very high work content, with tons of data available in the reports to help us with continuous improvement projects.
We are able to fully train shipping employees in around 2 hours now.
As an E-Commerce business analyst who is obsessed with data-driven business decisions and the tenets of Lean, I wholeheartedly recommend ShipHero!

classification.summary: Customer praises ShipHero's adaptability, customer service, and ability to centralize inventory management across multiple sales channels, but this is a review of a competitor product, not a prospect for Saltbox.

If you run a small business and need a pure shipping solution, shipstation is still a winner.  If you need expanded functionality (barcodes, inventory management, etc), look elsewhere
***********

We own our own store in addition to owning a 3PL that ships for other e-retailers. Moving to ShipHero has been one of the easiest and most pleasant experiences we've had in our years of existence.
Previously we had to manage inventory with one app and then ship with another. This meant we had to develop a lot of processes for returns, shipping, and inventory that jumped from platform to platform. Now we're able to handle it all in one place which as created so many efficiencies for our team.
Above all, their team is incredibly responsive to any issues that come up and they're very accommodating to features and requests that we've had. I really can't recommend them enough.

content.body:
September 9, 2015:WOW! What a difference a few months makes! So we had tried Shipstation before to have as a backup to our other shipping software (see original review below). It didn't work for us then but it works great now! Now our other shipping software is not working and we decided to see if Shipstation had fixed it's issues and boy is this good software. We immediately switched all our shippers to Shipstation. We love it and it saved us when we needed it the most. THANK YOU SHIPSTATION FOR TAKING OUR EXPERIENCE INTO CONSIDERATION AND FIXING YOUR SOFTWARE!! =)
April 27, 2015:The software looks very nice and sleek, and the customer service people are nice. I am very sure this company is comprised of lovely people so please understand this review is only about the functionality of the service/website. Where this website/service fails is if you are a volume seller. For example, if you have a large sale where you get 10s of thousands of orders at the same time, the software LITERALLY cannot find any order you look up to ship because naturally those orders will be "pending" shipping. A customer service rep at Shipstation confirmed this is a known issue with them and said they will "suggest" this be fixed. So I have to uninstall this app. If you have the potential sell 5K, 10K, 20K, 30K, 40K etc. orders at the same time this shipping service is not for you.

classification.summary: Morphe Inc experienced scaling issues with Shipstation's inability to handle large order volumes simultaneously, though they later returned after the software was improved.

content.body:
ShipStation was just great until a few months ago. Then things started going wrong. The app began to lose track of our scale at random times and could no longer read weights. At first we thought it was the computer we were using so we bought a new one but it did not help. I tried downloading ShipStation Connect and reinstalling it but it still did not fix it. So we decided maybe it was our scale and replaced that as well. All to no avail, so I went to the forum to see if there was anyone else having issues. I found not one but many others having the same problem with the printer or the scale.
That was not the worst of it though. They made a change to the weights and included a decimal place in the ounces. This caused errors in more than one area and I submitted a request to support to tell me how to remove the decimal from my set up. Well, I got lectures, articles and repeated explanations as to why I needed that decimal. I was told that there was no way the decimal could have caused any problems and that it was there to stay.
And that was that - we immediately began looking for a new app to replace ShipStation. Absolute worst customer service and support ever experienced.

classification.summary: Garden Tool Co is actively switching away from ShipStation due to technical issues with scale integration and poor customer support that refused to address their concerns about decimal place formatting.

content.body: Fulfillrite is possibly my biggest asset so far. As long as you follow receiving guidelines, they really are click and forget. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND THEM to anyone looking for fulfillment.

content.body: This app has definitely made shipping easier for us. The software allows auto import of customer data from many sites and shipping rates are automatically applied if filters and rules have been set up. However I'd caution anyone who is looking for shipping software to shop around. Shipstation offers no phone support, the software has difficulty automatically splitting applying shipping rates to orders containing multiple items, and the bulk import tool sucks. This means that if you have lots of customers that buy multiple items, you're still doing a lot of manual sorting. Also, if you run a Kickstarter campaign, for example, and need to import several hundred orders VIA .csv, you're going to run into problems.

content.body: I've been meaning to write this review for a little while now, but like any good love story, taking the time to come up for air has been hard. Also like any other crazy love story, all that comes to mind when I think about Shipstation is a bunch of corny cliches. I'm a stay at home mom of two little boys and started my own clothing line for new moms. I was sending a couple of orders a week to friends and family by going to the post office. Business picked up to four or five orders a week and I started printing shipping online. Then we ran our first ad on Facebook and things took off. Shipstation no joke changed my life. I have managed to keep up with over 200 orders a week (and keep my babies alive) by using the program. I can even make scrambled eggs and print postage at the same time. I can't get over how easily it works alongside Shopify and how easy it is to learn how how to use. Seriously if you are using anything else right now, stop- and bring Shipstation on board. Sometimes at the end of the night when I go to sleep all caught up on orders, I send a little virtual hug to Texas. Seriously, my company would still be a little hobby if it wasn't for Shipstation.

content.body: I looked for a long time for a program like Shipstation. I check orders in the morning, see how various shipping methods have been automatically applied, I can change a shipping speed, add a note, add a colored label to indicate a rush, or hold an order if a customer has a last minute change. Order cancellations are easy, and done by the time the fulfillment company fills the orders at 8:30.We make no more mistakes. content.body: After several years of being with an Atlanta-based fulfillment company that required orders be sent in manually and would provide tracking information that I had to manually send to individual customers, I decided enough was enough. Enough waking up at 5 a.m. to have orders submitted by 9 a.m. so they would be sent on the same day. After some research, I found Fulfillrite, talked to Joe Brieger who answered all my questions, and told me about using Shopify and the fulfillment app integration. I wish I had found this years sooner. It works seamlessly in the background, just collects orders and sends them to fulfillment, then sends the tracking information back to my customers without my having to do ANYTHING. I've freed up a minimum of 6 hours in my day, I no longer have to wake up at 5:00 a.m., and I'm free to focus on the more creative aspects of my company, which has expanded business. Because of this app integration, I have more time in my work day, more free time with my family, more money from increased business, and the quality of service and customer care from Fulfillrite is unmatched. Over the past few months, I've had NO problems, NO screwed up orders, NO angry customers. If you're on the fence about this, just DO IT. I barely even need my computer for work anymore - with this app running in the background handling fulfillment, and the actual Shopify dashboard app on my iPhone, I can handle 90% of my daily task on my iPhone, from anywhere in the world that I can get reception or find Wifi. 5 Stars, 10/10, A++, if you need fulfillment services for your company, use Shopify with the Fulfillrite app integration. Best business decision I've ever made.

classification.summary: A satisfied Fulfillrite customer is praising their automated fulfillment integration after switching from a manual Atlanta-based fulfillment company that required early morning order submissions.

content.body: Fulfillrite is already a great company and the addition of this Shopify app takes their service to the next level. No more manually inputting all my orders everyday.  Thank you Fulfillrite!

I have been working with Fulfillrite for the past several months, and I have been continuously impressed by all aspects of their service. The integration with Shopify is seamless and hassle free - with the click of a button my accounts were synched up. The dashboard is intuitive and easy to use, and the client service is outstanding.
Joe and his team have been responsive and helpful with answering questions, receiving my large shipments from overseas, and coming up with quick, creative solutions to any issues that arise. content.body:
I wasn't sure I was going to get much out of Shipstation- I already used Endicia for shipping and I had no complaints. But a friend talked me into trying it and I am SO GLAD I did. The initial setup was a little complicated (not difficult, it just took some time) but I once I had everything up an running- wow, what a difference. I've finally achieved the streamlined, organized, efficient shipping workflow I'd always thought was just a fantasy.
There are way more awesome features than I could list here, but a few of my favorites:
I love the way Shipstation syncs with my sales channels so orders get marked as shipped, tracking emails get sent, and I can manage everything from one convenient dashboard.
I love that I get an alert when I have multiple orders from the same customer, and Shipstation allows me to merge the orders to ship together.
I love automation rules! No more weighing packages. I can't believe I spent years doing this stuff manually. I had no idea. This is a game changer.
The one missing link for me is inventory management- this app does everything else and the info and sales channel integrations are all there already, so it's a natural next step. (I hear they may be working on adding it.)
On a final note- I really appreciate how affordable Shipstation is. This is an app that is extremely powerful and full of advanced level features, but they have a pricing structure that allows even a tiny business like mine to take advantage of them.
Bottom line: if you sell stuff online, you owe it to yourself to try out Shipstation. You don't even know what you're missing!

classification.summary: Customer is enthusiastically praising ShipStation's automation and workflow efficiency features after switching from Endicia, though they note inventory management as a missing feature.

content.body: We know it might sound like too much flattery to say that ShipStation saved our lives, but it's true! We ship a lot of small orders, many to overseas addresses. It's a snap to set up filters and automated tasks. Then, with the click of a single button, all labels & invoices come flying out - with customs forms already filled out! Plus, you can link shipping providers, and get commercial shipping rates. Win. Win. Win.

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipStation's efficiency for handling high volumes of small and international orders with automated processes and integrated shipping rates.

content.body: ShipStation paid for itself many times over in the first month of use.  Between the time saved, address verification, and direct to label printing, it has been invaluable.  Thank you!

The only complaint we have had is that there is no way to schedule shipping notifications to be sent later (and not right when the label is created).  If ShipStation could implement this, it would be so great!

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipStation but requesting a feature to schedule shipping notifications instead of sending them immediately upon label creation.

I actually started out using Ordercup, and then switched over.  There is NO comparison between the 2, Not to talk bad about them, but SS simply is a much more refined, easy to use, and simply more up-to-date system.

That in and of itself was nice, but what was better is I have built a great relationship with Chris and he does not mind it when I email him quick questions. classification.summary: Satisfied ShipStation user praising the platform's shipping management capabilities and customer support.

content.body:
I don't usually leave a comment unless I am overwhelmed with something that is worthwhile spending time to share with...  well.  ShipStaion is..
I have researched pretty much every option available in the market. Here are the main reasons why you want to adapt their service so that you can find more time of yourself or spend more important things that only you can do...
Perhaps, go to Santa Monica beach with your family. :)
1. None of softwares are perfect; however, ShipStation support-staffs understand the means of excellent service. They WILL be available when a problem happens. - that is all it counts in business.
2. They work well with "other" associated support companies such as StitchLabs, Carrier services, etc.  They don't 'dump' problems to others, but take it seriously as if it is their own problem.
3. The results by signing up with their service extends far more value than the committed monthly fee. Our company has saved up to 30-40% of labor time comparing from where we were without Shipstation. It has virtually eliminated the most of human errors and our inventories are now well managed.
HIGHLY RECOMMENDED. - Don't waste your time searching somebody else.
D.

classification.summary: A satisfied ShipStation customer shares a positive testimonial praising their support quality, integration capabilities, and significant labor cost savings (30-40%), recommending others adopt their service.

content.body: Fulfillrite is a wonderful company to work with. The integration between Shopify and Fulfillrite is always seamless, and the staff at Fulfillrite are always willing to answer our questions and provide help when needed. Any time an order is placed through our shop, it is fulfilled within 1 business day by Fulfillrite. We really could not ask for anything more out of this app.

classification.summary: A satisfied Fulfillrite customer is praising their seamless Shopify integration and fast 1-day fulfillment service.

content.body: Charlie and the team at Fulfillrite are always extremely helpful and on top of it!  Their app integrating with Shopify really makes the entire experience as simple and stress-free as possible.  It's great to have peace of mind when it comes to fulfillment and spend that time focusing on all the other things that come with running a shop.

classification.summary: Customer is praising Fulfillrite's fulfillment service and Shopify integration, expressing satisfaction with their current provider rather than seeking alternatives.

content.body:
We LOVE ShipStation and we especially love it in tandem with Stitchlabs (http://apps.shopify.com/stitch-labs) .  Shipstation is a stable, highly configurable, intuitive, robust, platform that *WAY* more than pays for itself in the time it saves (not to mention discounted USPS rates).
If you're copying and pasting address info, signing into multiple shipping sites, copying and pasting tracking numbers, and wondering if there's a quicker way, you've just found it.  Takes a little bit of time to set up your filters and rules, but it was time well spent.  Shipping time is *easily* halved (if not more) after setup.
I mentioned Stitchlabs.  These two integrations have completely changed the way we run our online business and I can't imagine our business without these two apps.  Shipstation and Stitchlabs work seamlessly together and pass all the important info all the way back to Shopify.  Huge power when combining these apps: reporting, inventory tracking, light bookkeeping, and shipping all dramatically improved.
Added bonus about using both integrations: it counts as ONE channel in Shipstation (even if you have multiple channels tied to Stitchlabs).
Highly recommended!

classification.summary: Customer is praising ShipStation and Stitchlabs for dramatically improving their shipping efficiency and business operations through automation and integration.

content.body:
Before using ShipStation we dreaded spikes in orders... ok, maybe dreaded isn't the right word (because ^ orders means ^ dollars), but we were delayed 1-2 days in getting orders out when they came in in waves.
Now that we have ShipStation, we don't use it every day, but it helps us stay on track when we get hit by a big wave of orders and that, on it's own, is more than enough for us to justify keeping it around.
For some people it's an every day solution, for us, it's our muscle (and we love it).

classification.summary: Company uses ShipStation to manage order fulfillment spikes and values it as a solution for handling high-volume order waves efficiently.

content.body: Before the ShipStation app, I was spending hours putting together labels. This has sped up my process immensely, and it lets me look at sales reports!

classification.summary: Customer is satisfied with ShipStation's efficiency in streamlining their label creation and order management process.

content.body: Great customer service. They want you to succeed at shipping your product efficiently.

classification.summary: Customer praising Saltbox's customer service and commitment to helping them ship products efficiently.

content.body:
Fulfillrite is not only an excellent "pick and pack" service center, but the integration with Shopify is flawless. In fact, you don't even see the integration, its all behind the scenes. You'll be contacted by a representative who walks you through the setup and logistics. There are no settings that get added to Shopify but rather a separate web portal to access the Fulfillrite control panel.
So far it's been working great!

classification.summary: Customer is praising Fulfillrite's pick and pack service and seamless Shopify integration, indicating satisfaction with their current 3PL provider.
